rationale: The cited paper is used in the core theory section to support the forced-versus-unforced replacement distinction, not just in a peripheral literature list. The citing authors then build their main justification mechanism on this distinction, arguing that consumers may behave carelessly to turn an unforced upgrade into a more justifiable forced replacement. Although the citation is grouped with Bayus (1988) and not tied to method reuse, it meaningfully anchors the conceptual framing of the hypotheses.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Grewal et al. (2004) as prior conceptual support for forced versus unforced replacement situations. It uses that framework as a basis for its own argument about upgrade-driven carelessness and justification.
